title: CSS Text Styling
content:
### CSS Text Styling

CSS provides extensive properties to style text.

---

### Text Color

```css
p {
  color: #333;
}
```

---

### Text Alignment

```css
text-align: left;      /* Default */
text-align: right;
text-align: center;
text-align: justify;   /* Spaced to both edges */
text-align-last: center; /* Last line alignment */
```

---

### Text Direction

```css
direction: ltr;   /* Left to right (default) */
direction: rtl;   /* Right to left */
unicode-bidi: bidi-override;
```

---

### Vertical Alignment

```css
vertical-align: baseline;  /* Default */
vertical-align: top;
vertical-align: middle;
vertical-align: bottom;
vertical-align: sub;       /* Subscript */
vertical-align: super;     /* Superscript */
```

---

### Text Decoration

```css
text-decoration: underline;
text-decoration: overline;
text-decoration: line-through;
text-decoration: none;      /* Remove link underline */

text-decoration-color: red;
text-decoration-style: wavy;
text-decoration-thickness: 2px;

/* Shorthand */
text-decoration: underline wavy red 2px;
```

---

### Text Transform

```css
text-transform: uppercase;     /* ALL CAPS */
text-transform: lowercase;     /* all lowercase */
text-transform: capitalize;    /* First Letter Capital */
text-transform: none;          /* Default */
```

---

### Text Spacing

```css
letter-spacing: 2px;      /* Space between characters */
word-spacing: 5px;        /* Space between words */
line-height: 1.6;         /* Line spacing (unitless is best) */
white-space: nowrap;      /* No line breaks */
text-indent: 30px;        /* First line indent */
```

> **Best Practice:** Use `line-height` without units (e.g., `1.6`) so it scales with font size.
